# Attaché — Secure AI Agent Deployment Platform
> Turnkey macOS deployment for OpenClaw agents with enterprise-grade security

Attaché is a deployment platform that turns a Mac mini into a hardened,
always-on AI agent workstation. It handles the infrastructure so you can
focus on what your agents do, not how they're secured.

## Documentation

- [Security Overview](https://docs.attache.dev/security): Trust model, threat landscape, how Attaché mitigates by design
- [Hardening Guide](https://docs.attache.dev/security/hardening): Gateway config, exec policy, four-tier autonomy model, credential management
- [Multiplayer](https://docs.attache.dev/security/multiplayer): Safe patterns for team and multi-agent access
- [Risk Register](https://docs.attache.dev/security/risk-register): Structured risk/mitigation matrix with CVE applicability
- [Audit Procedures](https://docs.attache.dev/security/audit): Automated and manual security checks, IOC detection, incident response
- [LLM Provider Data Handling](https://docs.attache.dev/security/llm-providers): Provider comparison, subscription tiers, data sovereignty
- [Credential Managers](https://docs.attache.dev/security/credential-managers): Comparison of 10+ credential managers, push approval support, platform recommendations
- [Architecture](https://docs.attache.dev/architecture): System design, Ansible automation, network topology
- [Memory](https://docs.attache.dev/memory): Agent memory system, persistence, knowledge management

## Machine-Readable Resources

- [Self-Audit Guide](https://docs.attache.dev/llm.txt): Feed this to an OpenClaw agent to audit its own security configuration. 10 sections, PASS/WARN/FAIL criteria, scoring rubric.

## Key Security Features

- Dedicated macOS hardware (Mac mini) with admin user locked down via exec allowlists
- Gateway bound to loopback; remote access via Tailscale only
- Token-based gateway authentication (not trusted-proxy)
- 1Password integration with scoped service account vaults
- Four-tier autonomy model: free / pre-approved / approve-once / always-approve
- Multiplayer agent patterns: personal + team agent separation with memory isolation
- Secrets proxy daemon for credential access control with DM approval flows
- Bloom filter credential scanning for outbound exfiltration detection (roadmap)
- OWASP Agentic Top 10 alignment

## Roadmap

- Bloom filter credential scanning (detects secrets in outbound commands)
- Tiered risk scoring with time-decay approval caching
- Supervisor agent evaluation (hybrid heuristic + LLM scanning)
- OS-level sandboxing via macOS seatbelt profiles
- NemoClaw / OpenShell compatibility

## Context

- Built by [Spantree](https://spantree.net), a Trifork company
- Powered by [OpenClaw](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw)
- Security docs aligned with [OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications (2026)](https://genai.owasp.org/resource/owasp-top-10-for-agentic-applications-for-2026/)

## Contact

- Docs: https://docs.attache.dev
- GitHub: https://github.com/Spantree/attache-platform
